________________________________________: Nothing is pretty easy to imagine, in my experience. To imagine, though. To comprehend it is a beast of an undertaking I'd rather leave to you. 23-03-2026
llllllllllllllllllll__lllllwfgllllllllll: I've always despised looking in mirrors. Sucking in the girth to look presentable, holding my breath until the result registers in my brain as my normal. I wish I could look at my body and see a person, deserving of love, without meaningful flaws at all. But that's not who I am. It's not going to work. I've settled for something simpler. I want to change the way I think, not the way I look. I've started to make progress; now, finally, I can look in the mirror, see all these imperfections and blisters and redness, and find it at least interesting. If not beautiful, I can be interesting. 24-03-2026
BBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBB: Today, I and a few friends gathered and discussed our favorite topics, including yaoi and K-Pop and participated in very uncouth activities. We had a project to cover, for a language class, in which we replicate a music video using American Sign Language. One person involved was remarkably capable of learning the proper order and sign parameters, even though she lacked any education in the language, as a Latin student. Once we prepared ourselves, by watching videos about the group we were to mimic, we drove out to a countryside road. We filmed, we signed, we acted out a car crash, and so forth. It is, of course, necessary for a person to wave to all they pass by. Interestingly, I have found that waving two-handedly with a big smile, perhaps leaning in and crouching slightly, is endearing enough from most people to ease tensions, with someone you can't immediately talk to, for example if someone encounters you along their private road. Then, we went to a gas station, an airport, filming our parts there, before making our way to a pizzeria. There, we filmed briefly, finishing the shoot, before ordering food and eating. We repeated a bit we created in the airport, in which my friend was holding a Monster Energy, and I grabbed him by the throat, saying that he was not allowed to bring drinks into the plane. We giggled about it incessantly. When we got home, my friend brought out a homemade cake, much to our collective delight. We ate, noting that the heap of sprinkles atop the cake tasted of joyous shards of glass, and moved on. Getting to my own home, I played with my bunnies, who I will now introduce as Hali, named after the mausoleum at Halicarnassus, and Giza, which you can assume the origin of. They have recently become far less skittish, allowing direct approach with minimal recoil, and passing by without backing away. I have rewarded them with vitamin cookies. Notably, I took one's away as to tease them, but they didn't notice fast enough to grow mad, only realizing long after it was out of sight and therefore untraceable back to me. They seemed to tear up, though not really, so I gave it back, and the poor thing seemed so enamored by the fact that they had received two cookies that day, though I had only regiven them their original cookie. Also, before I went to my friend's house, I had been messing about with Scratch. I find it very fun to create complex things with such simple tools. A few days before, I had created a tool to map 3D points to a perspective camera, which was delightfully successful. As of now, I have yet to figure out how to use this in any actual games, as I don't know how to cull any faces properly, breaking the whole thing. Today, I tested a markov chain tool, as I intended to design a behavioral loop for NPCs in my games. It has worked wonderfully so far, and now I am setting up evolving these chains. Anyway, I went to bed after I played with my bunnies, saying goodnight to my parents. I write from bed. Goodnight. 15-02-2026
FFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF: Today, I enjoyed a can of tea with my friend. I hung out at their house after playing rhythm games and eating a bear claw, at which time we shared memes and ate rice. We went on errands, first returning an item before going grocery shopping. Finally, we ate pasta and watched Jumanji, and I got to have another bear claw. I have lost passion for this diary entry. Goodnight. 21-02-2026
3333333333333333333333333333333333333333: Today, I started my excellent day with some stale donuts and a banana. The morning was hotter than usual, the start of an incredibly, unusually hot day for the start of spring. Normally I wear a hefty raincoat to hold all my stuff for the day, but as I didn't want to end up more cooked than a prawn, I swapped it out with a handbag. Also, I forgot to bring a headband to school, as I had intended to gift it to a friend of mine who might like it. You see, the day before, I had decided that while I may be forced to share a room with my little brother, I certainly do not have to live in their filth. So, I cleaned my room, threw out all the cardboard scraps and popsicle sticks, and rearranged the furniture to better follow the rules of Feng Shui; I moved my desk to a command position, but angled the monitor of my computer to minimize glare. I also decluttered my shelves and curated the appearance of my room to cater to my appreciations, such as posters of Hatsune miku (x1) and pictures of my bunnies (x2). I feel so joyous. Part of my routine, once I get home, is sitting down at my computer, and working on custom levels for an emulator of Project Sekai which I have been into recently. Today, however, I find a very large air conditioner shoved into the corner between my desk and the wall, moving my desk just enough to require the cables to be reconfigured before I could turn on the computer, after which I was shown the bios unexpectedly, sending me into a rather blind panic. I finally got the computer to turn on, before I was called into the living room, given a bowl of microwave curry, and made to watch Despicable Me. I never did finish my dinner. After that, my family and I went to my little brother's band recital, which I have no comments on. Aside, I wrote out a technological specification document for an assistant I've been looking for, one that can help me manage my executive dysfunction. I hope to have a sort of guiding voice in the near future, helping me along by being a teacher I can be instructed by throughout situations where there wouldn't be one. As I do hate AI, I have decided not to rely on a big tech company for my model, and have thrown one together myself in my computer. I hope to make good use of this. Lastly, I spent a good deal of time today on a zoom call with my friends. We discussed happenings, preferences, and yaoi, and long into the night, we signed off. That brings me to now, and as of very late, I say goodnight. 16-03-2026
IIGWmrrbgPgwwwgIccccggQ6M6200000IMHWWrrc: I've never been able to see well. It's like my left eye is the only one I have, my nose on the right of my vision. I have to focus on something to bring it into the center of my vision, to bring detail to the colorful blob in my view. I always loved colors though, so I'll say it pains me that there are so few colors in this library. I'd give up pixel spaee if I could show you the vibrant colors that bring my brain comfort, that have drawn out these feelings inside me that have still yet managed to pull out all of this vulnerability from me. Why do you search for all this? If you speed through this, you might end up finding a picture that looks like something, but won't you just click the button immediately, losing it forever? I don't know what you're so excited about. As for me, I'm excited for sleep. Goodnight. 25-03-2026
lt____h__FNVhxllhFBFhlVhtBRNBhV_tVN_FBNl: Yesterday, I had been sitting on the couch with my mother. Receiving a text from a friend, I spoke up; "Mom? Can I go to the beach?" I threw on an outfit with haste, forgetting sunscreen but putting on socks, before drifting out the door in my signature face mask. My friend's car was already outside my house, waiting. I had given them the wrong address, whether that be an accident, or an unconscious result of my reluctance to give out my address to anyone, knowing that would allow them to show up any time, to see the face I've worked so hard to hide for so many years, I wouldn't know. I climbed in, speaking briefly to their father, who was driving, before we set off. Along the way, we picked up another friend, whose neighborhood wasn't marked on the navigator. Yet again, we drove the car into the lot of a couple of apartments, as to pick up the final passenger, who brought with them an enormous beachball in a plastic crate. We hauled it into the back, and set out for the beach. One thing I'll note about the friend that picked me up; they live immensely far away, to the point that when I once went to their birthday party, we were driving so far out that we had gotten lost, and were now riding along a long, windy road in some strange hilly, dune-filled expanse. It was so strange, the navigator had switched from saying the route was two miles long to saying it was two-hundred miles long, just to get to their house. While our chaperone panicked, me and my friends joked about being followed by 'the cult.' Though a nameless, unspecific threat, we couldn't help but cough a bit at the idea. It was so very possible, so very unlikely, so very justified for this situation, for we knew that when we would eventually leave that party, we would be taking this route back in the dark. Finally arriving, we briefly played with the birthday-haver, before going outside to meet their dogs. One dog, Cricket, immediately jumped up onto me, playful, joyous as dogs are, but very aggressive in her complete inconsiderance of any boundaries I could possibly have. One moment I was having fun, and the next, my mask was ripped off. I took it back, covered my face with my hands, played it off as I retreated to the bathroom, puncturing holes in the corners of the cloth and threading the straps through, before tying them off. I donned the tattered thing, and returned to the party. Then, at the beach, we met up with many others, all from our peer space. We set up our things, and began to dig a hole. Then, as one stayed back, we all went to wade in the shallow river that fed into the beach. Unfortunately, it was quite boggy, and our feet kept sinking into the sand beneath the water, as we trekked to the other side. There, some (the men) climbed a short cliff, arguing that the rest of us were wusses for not joining them. I, for one, was in a sheer, delicate white dress, that was previously described as "dead wife esque," because it was faint and pale as is the typical flashback or hallucination of a late wife in standard cinema, entirely unfit for climbing. Speaking of, that dress was one I bought the day before that, on a trip to the thrift store. I had found this lovely skirt and dress, which when layered, made me feel so, so beautiful, like a stroke of white and blue fabric on the canvas that is my world. I wanted to wear both to the beach that day, but the blue skirt had been still in the wash when it was time to go. The outfit, which I'll also be wearing tomorrow, reminded me of a character I loved. The day before even that (two days before yesterday), I decided to binge watch items on my watchlist, and the first thing I cleared from my list was, well, Akiba maid war, but after that it was Violet Evergarden. From the first time I saw her, I had been enraptured. It didn't matter that she was thin and I wasn't, I loved how delicate yet strong she was, how beautiful she looked in her dress, how daintily she held that parasol, how elegant she was. I wanted that for myself, desperately, so that day, when I found a dress that made me feel like that, I fell in love. As we waded back, groaning and making sour faces, we reached our towels, before returning to the hole and some brownies one of us had brought along so generously. Speaking of this friend, while they were sitting by the hole, I glanced up and was horrified to see that they were being pelted with water from multiple waterguns, and then lastly hit with a lob of wet sand to the shoulder (the men had come back). Me and another friend helped them up, and down to the water to wash off. There, a wave came. It crashed over me, soaking my dress, my hair, and my mask. It was so hard to breath through so suddenly, like my mouth was gone, so I took it off. For the first time in years, I was out in public, without my mask. I wanted to leave. I didn't. I went back to the shore, and laid down in my towel, out of it but not yet broken. It took me a while, but I eventually went back out into the water, swimming half-heartedly. It the moment, it seemed a lot more fun. I can't describe what was fun about it now; it's just not an important memory, even if I loved the action that caused the memory. We dried off, and headed down the board walk, in search of sweets. We found a small store selling ice cream and shaved ice, and stopped by for a bite. I had forgotten to grab money before going out that day, so a friend offered to pay for me, sweet as they always are. Coincidentally, we ended up ordering the exact same things; a cup of Birthday Cake ice cream, one scoop. We recorded a video for our friend, who was in France then, which I think might be the only ever recording of my face beyond the age of 12. It didn't feel so hard like this. We went back to the group, and hung out for a while again, before me and another person went out to the restrooms so they could take care of business, and we discovered that there were gender-neutral bathrooms, which were equally unkempt. While I waited outside, I leaned over the railing, gazing out into the ocean. I felt a tap on my shoulder, and when I looked back, I saw a man holding a camera. He told me he wanted to take a picture, that he was a photographer. I didn't know what to do, but I nodded and resumed my pose. He took the shot, printed the result, and when I looked, I was shaken by how deeply I underestimated how aesthetic I could appear. My heart fluttered, though I might be exaggerating now, but I couldn't but find myself a little more impressive. He left, and my friend came out of the bathroom, and we walked back to the group. Along the boardwalk, there was a group of people having a barbecue, and they had a pack of water bottles. I called out, asking for one, and they offered to give both of us one each, to my appreciation, though my friend politely passed. Later, as my friend's mom came to pick us up, we loaded the crate into the back, shoved ourselves into the car, and drove off. I'm still dealing with the sunburns. 29-02-2026
